question_text: Describe one practical application of infrared radiation in everyday life.
correct_answer: Infrared radiation is used in thermal imaging cameras, heat lamps, or infrared thermometers to detect heat/temperature. (Accept: remote controls, oven grills, heat therapy, night vision)
explanation: Infrared radiation has wavelengths slightly longer than visible light and is felt as heat. Many devices use infrared to detect or emit heat energy in practical applications.
